core_behavior: The system must validate all user input data before processing it.
Validation will check for correctness, completeness, and consistency of the input data.
The system will ensure that the input data conforms to predefined rules, formats, and constraints.
inputs: Selection fields (e.g., dropdown menus, checkboxes, radio buttons)
outputs: Validation Success: If the input data is valid, the system will proceed with processing the input data.
Validation Failure: If the input data is invalid, the system will display an error message to the user, indicating the specific validation errors.
Error Codes: The system will log error codes for each validation failure, which can be used for debugging and auditing purposes.
assumptions: A set of predefined validation rules will be implemented, including but not limited to: data type checks (e.g., integer, string, date), format checks (e.g., email, phone number, credit card number), range checks (e.g., minimum and maximum values), regular expression checks (e.g., password strength).
The system will handle validation errors in a way that prevents data corruption and ensures data consistency.
The system will sanitize user input data to prevent SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.
failure_modes: Invalid Input: If the user provides invalid input data, the system will display an error message and prevent processing of the input data.
Validation Rule Errors: If a validation rule is incorrectly defined or fails to execute, the system will log an error and prevent processing of the input data.
System Errors: If a system error occurs during validation (e.g., database connection failure), the system will log an error and prevent processing of the input data.
edge_cases: Empty Input: If the user provides empty input data, the system will validate the input data as invalid.
Null Input: If the user provides null input data, the system will validate the input data as invalid.
Boundary Values: The system will validate input data at boundary values (e.g., minimum and maximum values).
Special Characters: The system will validate input data containing special characters (e.g., punctuation, whitespace).
security_constraints: The system must validate all user input data to prevent SQL injection and XSS attacks.
The system must sanitize user input data to prevent data corruption and ensure data consistency.
The system must handle validation errors in a way that prevents data corruption and ensures data consistency.
performance_constraints: The system must validate user input data within a reasonable time frame (e.g., less than 1 second).
The system must handle validation errors within a reasonable time frame (e.g., less than 1 second).
The system must utilize system resources (e.g., CPU, memory) efficiently to prevent performance degradation.
